title: grid-column
description: Controls which columns a grid item occupies. The span keyword is the most flexible form — span 2 means 'take up 2 columns from wherever I land', without counting exact grid line numbers.
html:
Featured
Card 2
Card 3
Card 4
Card 5
challenge: Make the featured card span two columns. The other cards should rearrange automatically around it.

alternatives.name: Explicit line numbers
alternatives.description: grid-column: 1 / 3 pins the item to specific column lines. Use this when the item must be at a precise position, not just any position that spans N columns.

alternatives.name: grid-row: span
alternatives.description: The same span syntax works for rows. grid-row: span 2 makes an item tall enough to occupy two rows — useful for vertical featured cards or sidebar items in a masonry-style layout.

realWorldUsage: Featured articles in news grids, wide call-to-action banners inside card grids, and dashboard stat cards that show more important metrics at wider widths — anywhere one item deserves more horizontal space than its siblings without breaking the surrounding layout.
